data.title: First Solar Rises After Earnings. Management Sees Hope in Darkening Solar Industry. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Avi Salzman

First Solar shares jumped 5% in after-hours trading shortly after the company reported better-than-expected second-quarter earnings and offered a relatively sunny outlook at a tough time for renewable energy.

The nation's largest solar manufacturer expects to be able to weather a policy environment that has turned against solar power.

"In our view, the recent policy and trade developments have, on balance, strengthened First Solar's relative position in the solar manufacturing industry," said CEO Mark Widmar.

First Solar benefits from some of the tariffs the Trump administration is placing on foreign solar providers, as well as from rules in the Republican tax bill punishing companies that use Chinese solar products. That said, the company could see a slump in demand for its products because the same bill cuts subsidies for solar power.

First Solar posted $3.19 of earnings per share, above expectations for $2.66.

The company's forecasts for its full-year results were a mixed bag. Management slightly increased its sales guidance slightly, but also raised its expectations for operating expenses. The midpoint of its range of forecasts for operating earnings is now slightly lower. And management is now predicting earnings per share of $13.50 to $16.50, compared with a forecast for $12.50 to $17.50.

First Solar had said on its first-quarter earnings call that some of the Trump tariffs would hurt its factories in places like India, and potentially force the company to idle overseas operations.

Write to Avi Salzman at avi.salzman@barrons.com

data.title: UPBOUND GROUP, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Upbound Group, Inc., a leading provider of rental and financial services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance, operational achievements, and strategic initiatives aimed at driving future growth. This comprehensive summary provides an overview of the key financial and business highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $1,157.5 million, reflecting an increase from $1,076.5 million in the prior year period, driven by growth in Rentals and fees, Merchandise sales, and the introduction of Subscriptions and fees.

Gross Profit: $571.8 million, up from $532.0 million in the previous year, indicating improved profitability.

Operating Profit: $50.7 million, a decrease from $80.7 million in the prior year, impacted by higher operating expenses.

Net Earnings: $15.5 million, down from $33.9 million in the previous year, primarily due to increased operating expenses and legal accruals.

Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $0.26, compared to $0.61 in the prior year, reflecting the decrease in net earnings.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reports revenue across four segments: Acima, Rent-A-Center, Brigit, and Mexico. Acima generated $618.97 million in revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2025, while Rent-A-Center contributed $467.12 million. The newly acquired Brigit segment added $51.89 million, and the Mexico segment accounted for $19.56 million.

Geographical Performance: The Mexico segment experienced a slight decline in revenue, generating $19.56 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $20.87 million in the same period of 2024. This indicates a need for strategic focus to enhance performance in this region.

New Product Launches: The acquisition of Brigit has introduced new financial health products and tools, such as Finance Helper, Earn and Save, Instant Cash, Credit Builder, and Identity Theft Protection, which are expected to enhance the company's service offerings and customer engagement.

Operational Efficiency: The company has been focusing on optimizing its operating expenses, with Rent-A-Center and Acima segments showing a reduction in operating labor costs compared to the previous year. This reflects efforts to streamline operations and improve cost management.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued integration and growth of the Brigit segment, leveraging its technology-driven financial solutions to expand its customer base. Additionally, the company is evaluating the impact of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act on its operations, which may influence future financial strategies.

SEC Filing: UPBOUND GROUP, INC. [ UPBD ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: Schneider National, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Schneider National, Inc., a leading provider of transportation and logistics services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements, driven by strategic acquisitions and robust segment performance. This summary provides an overview of the key financial metrics and business developments that have shaped the company's recent success.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating Revenues: $1,420.5 million, reflecting an 8% increase compared to the same quarter in 2024, driven by growth in Truckload and Logistics segments.
  • Income from Operations: $55.0 million, an increase of 8% from the previous year, attributed to increased volume in Dedicated and Intermodal segments.
  • Net Income: $36.0 million, a slight increase of 2% from the previous year, with a lower effective tax rate contributing to the improvement.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $0.21, reflecting a consistent performance compared to the previous year.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.20, maintaining the same level as the previous year, indicating stable earnings performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Truckload segment saw a 15% increase in revenues (excluding fuel surcharge) due to growth in Dedicated volume, primarily from the Cowan acquisition, and improved rates in both Dedicated and Network operations. The Intermodal segment experienced a 5% increase in revenues (excluding fuel surcharge) driven by a rise in volume. The Logistics segment's revenues (excluding fuel surcharge) increased by 7%, attributed to the Cowan acquisition, despite lower brokerage volume and revenue per order.
  • Sales Units: In the Truckload segment, Dedicated operations reported a 23% increase in volume, largely due to the Cowan acquisition, while Network operations saw a 6% decrease in volume. The Intermodal segment reported a 5% increase in orders, reflecting higher demand.
  • Operational Performance: The Truckload segment's income from operations increased by 31%, driven by higher revenues and offset by increased costs from the Cowan acquisition. The Intermodal segment's income from operations rose by 10%, benefiting from increased volume and reduced rail costs. However, the Logistics segment's income from operations decreased by 29% due to lower brokerage volume.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in its Dedicated operations, supported by the Cowan acquisition, and expects to leverage its scalable platform and experienced operations team to pursue further acquisitions that meet its strategic criteria. The company also plans to maintain its focus on balancing its transportation network and efficiently managing resources to meet customer demand.

SEC Filing: Schneider National, Inc. [ SNDR ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: QUAKER CHEMICAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Quaker Chemical Corp, a leading global provider of process fluids, chemical specialties, and technical expertise, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and strategic initiatives that have shaped the quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $483.4 million, an increase of 4% compared to $463.6 million in the second quarter of 2024, driven by acquisitions and increased sales volumes.
  • Gross Profit: $171.7 million, reflecting a slight decrease from $175.7 million in the second quarter of 2024, impacted by lower operating margins.
  • Operating (Loss) Income: $(52.5) million, compared to $58.4 million in the second quarter of 2024, primarily due to an $88.8 million non-cash impairment charge.
  • Net (Loss) Income: $(66.6) million, compared to net income of $34.9 million in the second quarter of 2024, largely due to the impairment charge.
  • Net (Loss) Income Per Share - Diluted: $(3.78), compared to $1.94 earnings per diluted share in the second quarter of 2024, reflecting the impact of the impairment charge.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's net sales for the second quarter of 2025 were driven by contributions from acquisitions (approximately 6%) and an increase in sales volumes (approximately 2%), partially offset by a decline in selling price and product mix (approximately 4%). The sales volumes increase was primarily due to growth in the Asia/Pacific segment and new business wins across all segments.
  • Geographical Performance: The Asia/Pacific segment showed continued growth, contributing significantly to the increase in sales volumes. However, the Americas and EMEA segments faced challenges due to soft end market conditions and uncertainty caused by tariffs.
  • Sales Units: The company experienced a 2% increase in sales volumes compared to the prior year, indicating a positive trend in operational performance despite challenging market conditions.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on delivering long-term financial and strategic initiatives despite the continuation of soft end market conditions and heightened uncertainty due to global trade and tariffs. The company is also assessing the potential impact of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBB) on its operations and financial statements.

SEC Filing: QUAKER CHEMICAL CORP [ KWR ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: Huron Consulting Group Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Huron Consulting Group Inc., a leading provider of business consulting services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, showcasing growth across various segments and strategic acquisitions that are expected to drive future growth.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $411.8 million, reflecting an increase from $381.0 million in the same period last year, driven by growth across all segments.

Operating Income: $45.7 million, a decrease from $59.4 million in the prior year, impacted by higher unallocated corporate expenses.

Net Income: $19.4 million, down from $37.5 million in the previous year, primarily due to increased interest expenses and other income (expense) net.

Net Income Per Basic Share: $1.12, compared to $2.10 in the prior year, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Net Income Per Diluted Share: $1.09, compared to $2.03 in the previous year, consistent with the decline in net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments:

  • The Healthcare segment reported revenues before reimbursable expenses of $197.8 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, driven by consulting and managed services, and digital offerings.
  • The Education segment generated $129.3 million in revenues before reimbursable expenses, with a strong contribution from digital services.
  • The Commercial segment saw revenues before reimbursable expenses of $75.4 million, with digital services showing significant growth.

Segment Performance:

  • The Healthcare segment's operating income increased to $59.7 million, reflecting improved operational efficiencies.
  • The Education segment achieved an operating income of $32.3 million, supported by strategic acquisitions and service expansions.
  • The Commercial segment's operating income was $12.5 million, highlighting growth in digital services.

New Acquisitions:

  • In March 2025, Huron acquired Advancement Resources and Halpin Partnership Limited, enhancing its Education segment offerings.
  • In June 2025, the acquisition of Eclipse Insights LLC bolstered the Healthcare segment's revenue cycle consulting capabilities.

Future Outlook:

  • The company anticipates continued growth in its Healthcare and Education segments, driven by recent acquisitions and expanded service offerings.
  • The Commercial segment is expected to benefit from the acquisition of TVG-Treliant Holdings, LLC, which will enhance its advisory and managed services capabilities in the financial services industry.

Operational Strategy:

  • Huron continues to focus on expanding its digital services across all segments, leveraging technology and analytics to drive client outcomes.
  • The company is also committed to enhancing its consulting and managed services through strategic acquisitions and organic growth.

data.title: CVR PARTNERS, LP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CVR Partners, LP, a leading producer of nitrogen fertilizer products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by favorable market conditions and strategic initiatives. The company has seen substantial growth in net sales, operating income, and net income, alongside robust production volumes and strategic operational enhancements.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $168.6 million, increased from $132.9 million in the prior year period due to favorable UAN and ammonia sales volumes and prices.

Operating Income: $46.3 million, up from $33.6 million in the prior year period, driven by higher revenues and lower pet coke feedstock costs.

Net Income: $38.8 million, compared to $26.2 million in the prior year period, reflecting improved market conditions and increased demand.

Basic and Diluted Earnings Per Common Unit: $3.67, up from $2.48 in the prior year period, reflecting higher net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Partnership's revenue is primarily derived from the sale of nitrogen fertilizer products, including ammonia and urea ammonium nitrate (UAN). For the three months ended June 30, 2025, UAN sales contributed significantly to revenue growth, with favorable sales volumes and prices. Ammonia sales also saw an increase in both volumes and prices.

Sales Units: For the three months ended June 30, 2025, the Partnership produced 197,000 tons of ammonia and 321,000 tons of UAN. The ammonia production was slightly lower compared to the previous year due to planned and unplanned outages, while UAN production remained robust.

Production Volumes: The Partnership's ammonia production for the six months ended June 30, 2025, was 413,000 tons, with 117,000 tons available for sale after upgrading into other products. UAN production for the same period was 668,000 tons, reflecting strong operational performance.

Operational Performance: The ammonia utilization rate decreased to 91% for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to 102% in the same period in 2024, primarily due to planned downtime and minor unplanned outages. However, the six-month utilization rate remained consistent at 96%.

Future Outlook: The Partnership is focused on several strategic initiatives, including the potential use of natural gas as an alternative feedstock at the Coffeyville Facility, which would make it the only nitrogen fertilizer facility in the U.S. with dual feedstock flexibility. Additionally, the Partnership plans to install a nitrous oxide abatement unit during the fourth quarter of 2025 to enhance environmental compliance.

Market Conditions: The Partnership anticipates continued strong demand for nitrogen fertilizers due to increased corn planting acreage and favorable market conditions. The USDA estimates a 4.9% increase in corn acres planted in 2025 compared to 2024, which supports the demand for nitrogen fertilizers.

Geopolitical and Regulatory Environment: The ongoing geopolitical tensions, including the Russia-Ukraine war, continue to pose risks to global markets, potentially affecting the production and trade of fertilizers. Regulatory changes, such as the EPA's renewable volume obligations, are expected to support demand for corn and, consequently, nitrogen fertilizers.

SEC Filing: CVR PARTNERS, LP [ UAN ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: BEAZER HOMES USA INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Beazer Homes USA Inc., a prominent homebuilder in the United States, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended June 30, 2025. The report highlights the company's financial and operational performance amidst challenging market conditions and weaker consumer sentiment. The following sections provide a detailed summary of the key financial and business highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $545.4 million, reflecting a decrease from $595.7 million in the prior year quarter, primarily due to challenging market conditions and weaker consumer sentiment.

Gross Profit: $72.6 million, down from $103.3 million in the prior year quarter, impacted by inventory impairment and abandonment charges.

Operating (Loss) Income: $(3.7) million, compared to $28.5 million in the prior year quarter, driven by lower gross margins and increased SG&A expenses as a percentage of revenue.

Net (Loss) Income: $(0.3) million, a significant decline from $27.2 million in the prior year quarter, influenced by the aforementioned factors.

Basic (Loss) Income Per Share: $(0.01), compared to $0.89 in the prior year quarter, reflecting the net loss and reduced revenue.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from three geographical segments: West, East, and Southeast. The West segment generated $329.1 million, the East segment $149.2 million, and the Southeast segment $67.1 million for the quarter ended June 30, 2025.

Geographical Performance: The West segment showed a decrease in operating income from $45.6 million in the prior year to $27.4 million, while the East segment increased from $9.2 million to $14.5 million. The Southeast segment experienced a decline, reporting a loss of $2.6 million compared to an income of $12.8 million in the prior year.

Sales Units: The company reported a decrease in net new orders to 861, down 19.5% from 1,070 in the prior year quarter. Orders per community per month were 1.7 compared to 2.4 in the prior year quarter.

Average Selling Price: The Average Selling Price (ASP) for homes closed during the quarter was $517.3 thousand, up 2.4% from $505.3 thousand in the prior year quarter, primarily due to changes in product and community mix in the East segment.

Operational Strategy: The company is focusing on deleveraging, growing book value per share, and positioning for long-term growth. It is prioritizing share repurchases and has slowed land spending to match current market conditions.

Future Outlook: The company aims to reduce its net debt to net capitalization ratio to the low 30% range by the end of fiscal 2027, achieve double-digit compound annual growth in book value per share through fiscal 2027, and reach more than 200 active communities by the end of fiscal 2027.

SEC Filing: BEAZER HOMES USA INC [ BZH ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: Columbia Sportswear Posts Higher 2Q Revenue On International Sales Amid Soft U.S. Market
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Columbia Sportswear posted a narrower second-quarter loss that beat Wall Street's expectations, driven by increasing international sales amid softer U.S. sales and tariff headwinds.

The outdoor clothing company reported a loss of $10.2 million, or 19 cents a share, compared to a loss of $11.7 million, or 20 cents a share, a year earlier. Analysts were expecting a loss of 23 cents.

Columbia posted $605.2 million in revenue, up from $570.2 million the year prior. Analysts were expecting revenue of $588.5 million. Top-line gains were fueled by a 26% increase in revenue from Europe, Middle East and Africa and 13% revenue increase in Latin America and Asia Pacific. Revenue from the U.S. fell 2% year-over-year amid increasing softness in the American market.

The company said it boosted net sales by including earlier Fall 2025 shipments to mitigate tariff impacts. "The apparel and footwear industry is facing increasing tariffs, on top of already high existing duties," said Chief Executive Tim Boyle.

Columbia guided for $904 million to $922 million in third-quarter revenue and $1 to $1.20 a share in third-quarter earnings. Analysts see revenue of $936.5 million and earnings of $1.31 a share.

The company forecast full-year revenue of $3.33 billion to $3.4 billion, which would be a -1% to 1% change from the prior year. Wall Street sees $3.4 billion in full-year revenue.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1640 ET - Figma priced shares at $33 a piece and closed its first trading day at $115.50, for a 250% pop. That means the company had the highest first-day gain ever for an IPO sized at $500 million or more, according to Renaissance Capital, a provider of pre-IPO research and IPO-focused ETFs. Figma's debut is "a shot in the arm for the IPO market," said Matt Kennedy, senior strategist at Renaissance. "So I now feel more confident that we'll have a fairly robust calendar in the fall, and an even bigger 2026 than we expected," he added about the slate of future IPOs. "Hype and FOMO have returned to the IPO market!" Renaissance said in a post on X earlier Thursday. Only two other companies with public offerings of that size had first-day trading increases of greater than 150% in the research firm's 30 years of records-- those are Circle, which went public earlier this year, and Palm, which listed in March 2000. (yuliya.chernova@wsj.com)

1505 ET - The breakout IPO of software company Figma speaks to the pent-up demand for new, high-growth software names on the public markets. That example is likely to get more private companies to go public and bring long-awaited liquidity to the venture market, a venture investor said. "The market is thirsty for high growth software companies, " said Tomasz Tunguz, general partner at Theory Ventures. "If you are a startup waiting to go public, your cost of capital is significantly less than you thought," Tunguz said. If more companies with strong financials follow Figma's suit, venture capital would benefit. "That would start to produce distributions and liquidity that we haven't seen in a long time, " Tunguz added. (yuliya.chernova@wsj.com)

1305 ET - EBay is continuing to use generative AI to drive personalized customer engagement, a move that's part of the online marketplace's broader push to engage inflation-weary shoppers who are cutting back on discretionary spending. The company in late 2024 introduced AI-generated subject lines and personalized customer relationship management emails in the U.S., which drove a more than 40% increase in quality visits compared with its prior approach, company executives say. EBay in recent months expanded this feature to the U.K. The company is pushing AI into its advertising as well, which saw first-party revenue grow 17% in 2Q. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

1258 ET - The U.S. housing market is cooling overall, Realtor.com says, but the pace and severity of the slowdown varies widely across regions. The southern and western U.S. are shifting decisively in favor of buyers, with rising inventory, deeper price cuts, and longer time on market. In contrast, conditions in the Northeast and Midwest remain much tighter. Active listings rose for the 21st straight month, homes took 7 days longer to sell than last year, and 33 of the 50 largest metros posted year-over-year price declines--including Austin, down 4.9%, Miami off 4.7%, and Chicago 4.4% lower. More than one in five sellers cut their asking price. Only 19 of the 50 largest U.S. metros currently have prices below July 2022, and all are located in the South and West. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1244 ET - Ebay executives say 2Q strength was partially driven by more favorable trends in the U.S., where consumer demand improved and tariff impacts were more muted than anticipated. The executives say eBay observed some "breakage in elasticity" related to tariffs and the potential de minimis exemption, which impacted Greater China and Japan sellers. Greater China sellers slowed direct shipped inventory into the U.S., which was partially offset by sellers making products available in countries like the U.K. and Germany. Chief Executive Jamie Iannone says eBay is not immune to the increased costs from tariffs, but the company believes it's "relatively resilient from that perspective more so than others." Ebay surges 18%. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

1227 ET - F5 is looking to the intensifying AI boom for significant revenue growth over the coming years, even if it isn't yet seeing the full potential in its current earnings. CEO François Locoh-Donou said in the company's earnings call Wednesday the company will benefit from increasing AI adoption by providing AI data delivery services, securing AI applications and balancing and optimizing traffic across and within AI data centers. The company said it is currently benefiting from indirect AI business, in which customers are expanding their data capacity to be used for AI but are not involved in AI development themselves. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1226 ET - Collectibles were once again eBay's largest contributor to year-over-year growth as interest in trading cards accelerated for the tenth straight quarter, eBay executives say on an analyst call. The online marketplace, which posted higher 2Q profit and revenue, say interest in Pokemon cards has surged among collectors helped by a strong slate of product releases. The company sees growth across all major trading card subcategories, with sports trading cards and "Magic: The Gathering" cards accelerating sequentially. Ebay says it remains confident in the durability of its trading cards category, despite varied product release cycles. Ebay jumps 18%. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

1136 ET - F5's 16% growth in 3Q software sales was outshone by its 39% growth in hardware. That gives some cause for concern, say Raymond James analysts in a research note, adding that "a mix shift to software is necessary to support multiple expansion." F5's mid-single-digit growth outlook full-year software sales is disappointing, according to the analysts, who had expected double-digit growth for the segment. In its earnings call Wednesday, the company said some customers were opting for hardware where they previously would've bought software because they want more data in hardware on-premise instead of in public clouds due to increasing global regulation. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1123 ET - The U.K. competition watchdog's investigation into the evolving cloud market lacks the urgency needed to let smaller players thrive, says Mark Boost, chief executive of U.K. cloud-software provider Civo. The CMA on Thursday concluded its yearslong probe into the U.K. cloud sector with a recommendation that it investigate Microsoft and Amazon Web Services under its new tech competition law. It also honed in on Microsoft's licensing practices, saying they adversely affect the companies' rivals. "The CMA has identified the same issues but failed to follow through with the urgency that the market needs," Boost says. "The recommendation to refer AWS and Microsoft to the Digital Markets Unit (DMU) sounds strong, but without interim remedies or a clear timeline, it leaves dominant providers free to continue business as usual," he adds. (edith.hancock@wsj.com)

0847 ET - Be Semiconductor's recovery visibility remains limited, HSBC analysts write in a note. "While we acknowledge the company's structural growth drivers, we continue to think that the addressable market for advanced chip-connecting tools is unlikely to meet the company's expectations," they say. The U.K. bank cuts its net income forecasts for 2025 and 2026 by almost 28% and nearly 18%, respectively. Nonetheless, management's commentary around a potential recovery in orders in the third quarter is seen as encouraging as the bank anticipates a demand rebound in 2026. However, they flag remaining uncertainty around capex plans and the speed at which customers are adopting advanced chip-connecting tools. Shares are down 3.5% at 118.05 euros.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0518 ET - The cost of insuring euro-denominated junk bonds against default falls as market sentiment improves following strong quarterly results from Microsoft and Meta. "Stellar results from Microsoft and Meta have fired up investors, quickly shifting the focus from U.S. interest rates potentially staying higher for longer, to an environment where big tech is ruling the roost again," AJ Bell's Russ Mould says in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index which tracks euro junk bond credit default swaps, falls 2 basis points to 265bps, matching a more than three-year low reached earlier this week, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. Credit default swaps act as insurance against default on the underlying bonds.(miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0512 ET - China's robotaxi fleet could reach 300,000 to 500,000 vehicles by 2030, according to Barclays analysts. More than 500 million people in the country use ride-hailing services, completing about 16 billion trips a year, they note. Ride-hailing and taxi services account for a roughly equal share of the on-demand transportation market, they add. Robotaxis compete in the on-demand transport market of higher-tier Chinese cities, which includes 4.5 million to 5 million cars, the analysts write in a note. The robotaxi unit economics in the country may already be close to or at break-even today, excluding the upfront development costs, they say. Barclays estimates the current cost of making a robotaxi in China at about $35,000 to $40,000. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1258 ET - The U.S. housing market is cooling overall, Realtor.com says, but the pace and severity of the slowdown varies widely across regions. The southern and western U.S. are shifting decisively in favor of buyers, with rising inventory, deeper price cuts, and longer time on market. In contrast, conditions in the Northeast and Midwest remain much tighter. Active listings rose for the 21st straight month, homes took 7 days longer to sell than last year, and 33 of the 50 largest metros posted year-over-year price declines--including Austin, down 4.9%, Miami off 4.7%, and Chicago 4.4% lower. More than one in five sellers cut their asking price. Only 19 of the 50 largest U.S. metros currently have prices below July 2022, and all are located in the South and West. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

0931 ET - Schroders's results were at least in line with market expectations and in many areas much better, Panmure Liberum says in a research note. The U.K. asset manager posted net inflows and positive market movements for the first half, which lifted its assets under management to 670.6 billion pounds. "The market has been churlish in grasping the fundamental changes being undertaken at Schroders, both in respect of expected profits and also the multiple which is applied to estimated earnings; we see upside in both," the analysts write. They add that the group is running comfortably ahead of schedule to remove costs. Shares fall 0.5% to 388 pence and are up 20% year to date. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0925 ET - Just Group shareholders should be supportive being taken over at a very attractive premium, Jefferies says in a research note. The U.K. retirement-income products and services provider agreed to be bought by Brookfield Wealth Solutions for 2.4 billion pounds. "In our view, it is unlikely that Just Group's shareholders will achieve better value from either another strategic buyer, or as a separately listed company in the short-to-medium term," analyst Philip Kett writes. The 220 pence-a-share bid implies a 75% premium to Wednesday's closing price and a 29% premium to the stock's record high in April 2016, he adds. Shares soar 68% to 212 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0912 ET - St. James's Place's better-than-expected inflows, funds under management and cash profits for the first half helped shares soar to their highest level since March 2023. There was a fear that pricing changes would overshadow these results, but the opposite happened, Panmure Liberum says in a research note after the U.K. wealth manager's results and provision release. "The level of business production is a clear reminder that pricing is not the be-all-and-end-all for clients that it can be for some in the stock market; advice and relationships matter and St Jimmy's leads the way," analysts write. Shares are up 9.3% at 1,278.5 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0737 ET - Cigna Group reports fewer total pharmacy customers at the end of 2Q than the prior year, down 0.5% to 121.9 million. Total medical customers declined 5.2% to around 18 million. Behavioral care customers rose to about 23.9 million from 23.8 million and dental edged up to 18.4 million from 18.3 million. Cigna's customer relationship counts are closely watched as healthcare and insurance businesses navigate higher medical costs, with some cutting their forecasts, in part due to a shifting membership base. Cigna ticks up 2% premarket. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0655 ET - Prudential PLC appears to have saved hundreds of millions of dollars by settling a legal dispute with a shareholder in one of its main Malaysian businesses, Jefferies says in a research note. The group unexpectedly announced it settled with Detik Ria, who had started proceedings in April and sought $833 million in unpaid dividends plus interest. The settlement shrank the cost to one-tenth of the original amount, saving Prudential at least $717 million--or 2% of its market capitalization--analysts write. "Given that this calculation does not factor in the potential interest that has now been forgone, and that the settlement de-risks both the stock and the use of the proceeds from the impending IPO of ICICI Prudential Asset Management, we are not surprised [the] stock has risen," they add. Shares in London rise 2% to 957.2 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0500 ET - London Stock Exchange Group's results offered no extra surprises beside the bigger share buyback, AJ Bell's Russ Mould says. The stock-exchange and financial-information company announced plans for a further 1 billion pound share buyback over the coming months. The stock still seems fairly valued based on earnings, Mould adds. This is an important point, even after the recent 20% drop from its peak, he says. Shares are down 4.6% at 95.90 pounds. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0442 ET - London Stock Exchange Group's first-half performance is likely to lead to positive revisions to consensus estimates, JP Morgan Cazenove analysts write in a note. The stock-exchange and financial-information company delivered market-beating results and announced plans for a 1-billion-pound share buyback in the second half. The earnings beat, coupled with the larger-than-expected buyback, could lead to a low-to mid-single digit increase in consensus earnings per share, they say. However, the analysts also note a decline in annual subscription value, citing increased competition as a contributing factor. Shares are down 4.1% at 96.46 pounds. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0402 ET - London Stock Exchange Group delivered a market-beating performance in the first half, Morgan Stanley analysts Bruce Hamilton and Marina Massuti write in a note. Both earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization and pretax profit came ahead of consensus, they note. Additionally, the planned 1-billion-pound share buyback should offer some relief following the underperformance in annual subscription value growth, a key concern, they say. Shares are down 0.9% at 99.62 pounds. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0350 ET - London Stock Exchange Group's slowdown in annual subscription value is likely to raise concerns, RBC Capital Markets analysts Ben Bathurst and Jude Neanor write in a note. The stock-exchange and financial-information company's annual subscription value growth slowed to 5.8% in June from 6.4% in the first quarter, they say. This is partly attributed to a higher rate of cancellations, which reflects competitors' responses, presumably through price reductions, they add. "[This] is likely to attract attention and spark new debate around the competitive dynamics for the Data and Analytics business," they say. Shares are down 0.05% at 100.05 pounds. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0323 ET - The focus of Banco Comercial Portugues's call with analysts is likely to be on its net interest income outlook, particularly in Poland, Citi says in a research note after the lender's second-quarter print. The Portuguese bank owns a controlling interest in Polish lender Bank Millennium, and investors will focus on the expectations for its top line given the country's interest-rate outlook, analysts write. Attention will also be on the potential uses or distribution of excess capital given the stronger-than-expected capital generation in the second quarter, they say. BCP's pretax profit surpassed consensus expectations by 15% but this was a low-quality beat since it was driven by better other revenue and lower provisions, which included several one-offs, Citi says. Excluding these, the beat would have been 4%. Shares rise 1.7%. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0320 ET - Mediobanca's results don't seem to move the needle much, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says in a research note. The Italian bank reported fourth-quarter earnings that were fairly dull overall, with misses on net interest income and fees being partially offset by lower provisioning, analysts Hugo Cruz and Ben Maher write. Much of the guidance was already known following its recent investor day, they say. The lender's outlook for fiscal 2026 is broadly in line with consensus expectations, they add. Shares open roughly flat at around 19.4 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1528 ET - Oil futures snap a three-day winning streak with the approach of the U.S. tariff deadline and the OPEC+ meeting that's expected to bring another large output increase for September. A rash of U.S. trade deals, and threats of Russia sanctions have oil prices on track for solid weekly gains. If the sanctions are avoided, "if it's business as usual, the fundamentals for oil should weaken," says Neil Crosby, of Sparta Commodities. "A lot of people are expecting weaker demand going forward, OPEC are due to export probably a lot more oil after summer, and with those two things combined you should start to see more of these inventory builds." WTI settles down 1.1% at $69.26 a barrel. September Brent goes off the board at $72.53, down 1%. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1112 ET - Shell is using its operational and financial strength to weather challenging macroeconomic conditions, Quilter Cheviot's Maurizio Carulli writes. While earnings in its key liquefied natural gas division dropped 30%, it won't be too much of a concern for investors, Carulli writes. The unit will bounce back once prices begin to recover, Carulli adds. Shares trade up 1.6% at 2,722 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0918 ET - Oil futures are lower after a three-day rally driven by a spate of U.S. trade deals ahead of Friday's tariff deadline and threats of sanctions against Russia and buyers of its oil. If substantial tariffs are added to Russian oil buyers like India, the market reaction will "needless to say be hefty," Neil Crosby of Sparta Commodities says in a note. While chances are the threats aren't fully carried out, or not for long, replacing Russian crude would fall predominantly on the Dubai market, with more demand also for West African crude, WTI "and potentially some other European grades," he says. WTI is off 0.7% at $69.53 a barrel. September Brent is off 0.8% at $72.68. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0415 ET - Shell's second-quarter performance was notably resilient and was closer to that of its U.S. peers than its more volatile British counterpart, BP, investment platform eToro's Mark Crouch writes. Shell's decision to focus on oil and gas appears justified given its robust profitability and attractive returns, Crouch adds. Its pragmatic stance on hydrocarbons seems to be paying off, he adds. Shares trade up 2.4% at 2744 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0400 ET - Veolia's improved performance in key divisions such as water technologies remains encouraging, RBC Capital Markets analyst Joseph Pepper says in a research note. The French resource-management company's division saw a 5.4% revenue growth in the second quarter, compared to a weak performance the prior quarter. Overall, first-half results are in line, with the reiteration of full-year guidance of 5% to 6% Ebitda growth, in line with analysts' expectations. Shares trade 1.7% lower at 29.72 euros. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0349 ET - Shell's operating cash flow pre-working capital beat consensus expectations by 22%, Jefferies analysts Giacomo Romeo and Kai Ye Loh write. This is mostly due to a $1.7 billion boost from its joint ventures, they write. Thursday's earnings call with management is likely to focus on buyback resiliency, LNG Canada and cost reductions, they say. Shares trade up 2.4% at 2,744 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0334 ET - Shell's quarterly free cash flow rising by $1.2 billion despite lower commodity prices, a weaker trading result and unplanned downtime at its chemical plants is a decent outcome, Hargreaves Lansdown's Derren Nathan writes. The uptick in free cash flow, however, was not good enough to prevent a further rise in net debt, he adds. While its balance sheet is a key strength, investors may start to get nervous if net debt continues to rise for a prolonged period, Nathan adds. Thankfully for Shell, there are some signs that its financial performance could improve in the third quarter, he adds. Shares trade up 2.65% at 2,748.5 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0324 ET - Shell's second-quarter earnings were strong and contained no dramas, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. Shell's marketing division performed particularly well and delivered its best second-quarter for several years, they write. The British energy major maintained its $3.5 billion buyback, which isn't surprising given its stronger balance sheet comparative to many of its peers, they write. Shell's forward-looking guidance for its upstream division looks slightly better than current consensus expectations, but slightly worse than consensus for its LNG division, they write. Shares trade up 2.3% at 2,742 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

2108 ET - Dialog Group's 25-year terminal deal with Pengerang Biorefinery may boost annual profits by 10 million ringgit-15 million ringgit from FY 2029, says Maybank IB analyst Jeremie Yap in a note. He views the deal positively, ending Dialog's Pengerang area dry spell since 2019. To lift its share price, Dialog needs to secure more major tank terminal projects to grow recurring income, he reckons. One potential upside is securing a project at ChemOne's planned Pengerang Energy Complex, which may require long-term product storage, he adds. Maybank maintains a buy rating on Dialog and keeps its target price at MYR2.34. Shares are 1.2% higher at MYR1.71.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Coinbase misses the mark on quarterly sales, including this all-important revenue stream
data.description:
By Claudia Assis

Coinbase Global Inc. late Thursday reported a 26% drop in quarterly revenue, including lower-than-expected transaction revenue, and offered an outlook for its subscription revenue that also disappointed investors.

Coinbase (COIN) called its second quarter "solid" amid lower volatility. It reported total revenue of $1.5 billion, down 26% quarter on quarter. Analysts polled by FactSet expected sales of $1.59 billion.

Transaction revenue dropped 39% quarter on quarter to $764 million, versus expectations for about $814 million, according to FactSet.

The stock was recently down more than 7% in the extended session after paring some of its initial after-hours losses. It ended the regular trading day essentially flat.

Coinbase's subscription and services revenue was 6% lower quarter on quarter at $656 million, and also missed expectations of $708 million.

The crypto exchange's operating expenses grew by $193 million, or 15% quarter on quarter, to $1.5 billion, driven by $307 million in expenses related to the data-theft incident the company disclosed in May.

Coinbase guided for transaction revenue of $360 million in July, without providing guidance for the entire current quarter, when consensus calls for transaction revenue of $866 million.

It guided for third-quarter subscription and services revenue between $665 million and $745 million, thanks mostly to "higher average" crypto prices and stablecoin revenue, it said.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet are modeling subscription and services revenue of $753 million for the current quarter.

Coinbase ended the quarter with $9.3 billion in dollar resources and $1.8 billion in crypto assets held for investment, it said.

The company hailed "significant progress in policy and regulatory clarity," including the Genius Act recently signed into law, and the passage in the House of Representatives of the Clarity Act.

These "create a robust, comprehensive regulatory framework for stablecoins and tokenized assets, potentially unlocking new opportunities for Coinbase and solidifying U.S. leadership in digital finance," the company said.

Coinbase beat FactSet consensus regarding its GAAP earnings by a wide margin, reporting profit of $5.14 a share versus expectations of GAAP earnings of $1.25 a share on sales.

In the year-ago quarter, Coinbase reported GAAP earnings of 14 cents on sales of $1.45 billion.

-Claudia Assis

data.title: Broadway Financial Corporation Announces Results of Operations for Second Quarter 2025
data.description:
(20:50 GMT) *Broadway Financial 2Q Loss/Shr 2c >BYFC
(20:50 GMT) *Broadway Financial 2Q Net $603,000 >BYFC
(20:50 GMT) Press Release: Broadway Financial Corporation Announces Results of Operations for Second Quarter 2025

LOS ANGELES, July 31, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Broadway Financial Corporation ("Broadway", "we", or the "Company") , parent company of City First Bank, National Association (the "Bank", and collectively, with the Company, "City First Broadway"), reported consolidated net income before preferred dividends of $603 thousand, or $0.07 per diluted share, for the second quarter of 2025, compared to consolidated net income of $269 thousand, or $0.03 per diluted share, for the second quarter of 2024. Net loss attributable to common stockholders was $147 thousand during the second quarter of 2025 after deducting preferred dividends of $750 thousand, compared to net income attributable to common stockholders of $269 thousand for the second quarter of 2024. Diluted loss per common share was ($0.02) for the second quarter of 2025, compared to $0.03 of income per diluted common share for the second quarter of 2024. Diluted loss per common share for the second quarter of 2025 reflects preferred dividends of $0.09 per diluted common share.

For the first six months of 2025, the Company reported consolidated net loss before preferred dividends of $1.3 million, or ($0.15) per diluted share, compared to consolidated net income before preferred dividends of $105 thousand, or $0.01 per diluted share, for the first six months of 2024. Net loss attributable to common stockholders was $2.8 million during the first six months of 2025 after deducting preferred dividends of $1.5 million, compared to net income attributable to common stockholders of $105 thousand for the first six months of 2024. Diluted loss per common share was ($0.32) for the first six months of 2025, compared to $0.01 per diluted common share for the first six months of 2024. Diluted loss per common share for the first six months of 2025 reflects preferred dividends of $0.18 per diluted common share.

Second Quarter 2025 Highlights:

  • The net interest margin increased by 22 basis points to 2.63% for the second quarter of 2025, compared to 2.41% for the second quarter of 2024. This increase was driven largely by growth in the yield on average loan balances and a reduction in the cost of interest-bearing liabilities
  • Total deposits increased by $53.5 million, or 7.2%, during the first six months of 2025 compared to December 31, 2024
  • Capital ratios remain strong with a Community Bank Leverage Ratio of 15.69% at June 30, 2025 compared to 13.96% at December 31, 2024
  • Credit quality remains strong with non-accrual loans to total loans at 0.42% and non-performing loans to total assets at 0.36%
  • Borrowings were $69.2 million at June 30, 2025 compared to $195.5 million at December 31, 2024, a reduction of $126.3 million, or 64.6%

Chief Executive Officer, Brian Argrett commented, "We had a favorable second quarter of 2025, and continue to build on this positive momentum. Deposits grew by 2.9%, or $22.4 million, since March 31, 2025 and 7.18%, or $53.5 million, this year. We reduced borrowings by $126.3 million to $69.2 million as of June 30, 2025 resulting in lower cost of funds. The net interest margin was 2.63% for the three months ended June 30, 2025, which is an improvement of 22 basis points compared to the same three-month period of last year."

"Our results for the second quarter of 2025 were positively impacted by a reduction in non-interest expense of 26.23%, or $2.7 million, since last quarter, mainly due to the operational loss associated with the $1.9 million fraudulent wire during the first quarter, which will result in a corresponding gain if recovered. In addition, our second quarter financial results were positively impacted by a reduction in the provision for loan losses of $266 thousand, mainly due to a decrease in loans."

"We remain focused on executing our strategic goals and mission objectives, building a stronger balance sheet and improving profitability in order to drive long-term performance that will help support growth in the low-to-moderate income communities within our markets."

"As always, I thank our employees for their endless dedication and our stockholders, depositors, and board for their continued support of our strategy and mission. Your support and efforts are essential in our ability to improve our efficiency and promote growth."

Income Statement

  • Net Interest Income before provision for credit losses for the second quarter of 2025 totaled $7.8 million, representing a decrease of $163 thousand, or 2.1%, from net interest income before provision for credit losses of $7.9 million for the second quarter of 2024. The decrease resulted from a $1.3 million decrease in interest income, primarily due to a decrease in interest on interest-bearing deposits, as a result of a decrease in the average balance of interest-bearing deposits, as well as a decline in interest income on available-for-sale securities due to a decrease in the average balance of available-for-sale securities. These decreases were partially offset by a $1.1 million decrease in interest expense due to a decline in interest on borrowings as a result of a decrease in the average balance of borrowings. The Company reduced borrowings to improve the net interest margin and to support capacity for future loan growth.The net interest margin increased to 2.63% for the second quarter of 2025 from 2.41% for the second quarter of 2024, due to an increase in the average rate earned on interest-earning assets, which increased to 4.83% for the second quarter of 2025 from 4.71% for the second quarter of 2024, and a decrease in the cost of funds, which decreased to 3.07% for the second quarter of 2025 from 3.19% for the second quarter of 2024.Net Interest Income before provision for credit losses for the first six months of 2025 totaled $15.8 million, representing an increase of $358 thousand, or 2.3%, from net interest income before provision for credit losses of $15.4 million for the first six months of 2024. The increase resulted from a $2.0 million decrease in interest expense due to a decline in interest on borrowings as a result of a decrease in the average balance of borrowings. The Company reduced borrowings to improve the net interest margin and to support capacity for future loan growth. This increase was partially offset by a $1.7 million decrease in interest income, primarily due to a decrease in interest on interest-bearing deposits, as a result of a decrease in the average balance of interest-bearing deposits, as well as a decline in interest income on available-for-sale securities due to a decrease in the average balance of available-for-sale securities.The net interest margin increased to 2.67% for the first six months of 2025 from 2.34% for the first six months of 2024, due to an increase in the average rate earned on interest-earnings assets, which increased to 4.83% for the first six months of 2025 from 4.59% for the first six months of 2024, and a decrease in the cost of funds, which decreased to 3.02% for the first six months of 2025 from 3.11% for the first six months of 2024.
  • Recapture of/Provision for Credit Losses resulted in a recapture of credit losses of $266 thousand for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a provision for credit losses of $494 thousand for the three months ended June 30, 2024. This recapture was mainly due to the decrease in loans.The Provision for Credit Losses was $423 thousand for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $754 thousand for the six months ended June 30, 2024. There were no loan charge-offs recorded during the six months ended June 30, 2025 or 2024.The allowance for credit losses ("ACL") increased to $8.6 million as of June 30, 2025, compared to $8.1 million as of December 31, 2024. The Bank had four non-accrual loans at June 30, 2025 with an unpaid principal balance of $4.0 million. Credit quality remains strong with non-accrual loans as a percentage of total loans at 0.42% and non-performing assets to total assets of 0.36% despite the increase in non-accrual loans.
  • Non-interest Expense was $7.5 million for the second quarter of 2025, compared to $7.3 million for the second quarter of 2024, representing an increase of $242 thousand, or 3.3%. The increase was primarily due to increases of $224 thousand in professional services and $112 thousand in information services, partially offset by a $60 thousand decrease in supervisory costs and a $57 thousand decrease in compensation and benefits expense.Non-interest Expense was $17.7 million for the first six months of 2025, compared to $15.1 million for the first six months of 2024, representing an increase of $2.6 million, or 17.4%. The increase was primarily due to a $1.9 million loss incurred from wire fraud, which will result in a gain if recovered, as well as an $830 thousand increase in compensation and benefits expense. The increase in compensation and benefits expense was primarily attributable to the addition of full-time employees during 2024 in various production and administrative positions as part of the Bank's efforts to expand its operational capabilities to grow its balance sheet. These increases were partially offset by a $485 thousand decrease in professional services expense.
  • Income Tax Expense was $257 thousand for the second quarter of 2025 compared to $146 thousand for the second quarter of 2024. The increase in tax expense reflected an increase of $437 thousand in pre-tax income between the two periods. The effective tax rate was 30.09% for the second quarter of 2025, compared to 35.01% for the second quarter of 2024.The Company recorded an income tax benefit of $435 thousand for the first six months of 2025 and income tax expense of $89 thousand for the first six months of 2024. The decrease in tax expense reflected a decrease of $1.9 million in pre-tax income between the two periods. The effective tax rate was 25.60% for the first six months of 2025, compared to 50.28% for the first six months of 2024.

Balance Sheet

  • Total Assets decreased by $76.3 million at June 30, 2025, compared to December 31, 2024, reflecting decreases in cash and cash equivalents of $31.9 million, securities available-for-sale of $25.9 million, net loans of $11.6 million and FHLB stock of $5.9 million. The reduction in securities available-for-sale was mainly due to maturities and paydowns, and the cash from the securities in addition to the cash on hand was used to reduce borrowings, leading to the decrease in stock held with FHLB.
  • Loans Held for Investment, Net of the ACL, decreased by $11.6 million to $957.3 million at June 30, 2025, compared to $968.9 million at December 31, 2024. The decrease was primarily due to loan payoffs and repayments.
  • Deposits increased by $53.5 million, or 7.2%, to $798.9 million at June 30, 2025, from $745.4 million at December 31, 2024. The increase in deposits was attributable to an increase of $67.7 million in certificates of deposit accounts, partially offset by decreases of $4.5 million in savings deposits, $3.5 million in Certificate of Deposit Registry Service ("CDARS") deposits (CDARS deposits are similar to ICS deposits, but involve certificates of deposit, instead of money market accounts), $3.3 million in liquid deposits (demand, interest checking, and money market accounts), and $2.9 million in Insured Cash Sweep ("ICS") deposits (ICS deposits are the Bank's money market deposit accounts in excess of FDIC insured limits whereby the Bank makes reciprocal arrangements for insurance with other banks). As of June 30, 2025, our uninsured deposits, including deposits from City First Bank and other affiliates, represented 35% of our total deposits, compared to 32% as of December 31, 2024. We leverage our long-standing partnership with IntraFi Deposit Solutions to offer deposit insurance for accounts exceeding the FDIC deposit insurance limit of $250,000.
  • Total Borrowings decreased by $129.1 million to $133.0 million at June 30, 2025, from $262.1 million at December 31, 2024, primarily due to a $135.3 million decrease in FHLB advances, partially offset by a $9.2 million increase in secured borrowings related to participation loans.

Asset Quality

  • Allowance for Credit Losses was 0.89% of total loans held for investment at June 30, 2025, compared to 0.83% at December 31, 2024.
  • Nonperforming Assets were $4.4 million at June 30, 2025, compared to $264 thousand at December 31, 2024.

Capital

  • Stockholders' equity was $285.5 million, or 23.3% of the Company's total assets, at June 30, 2025, compared to $285.2 million, or 21.9% of the Company's total assets, at December 31, 2024.
  • Book Value per Share was $14.74 at June 30, 2025, compared to $14.82 at December 31, 2024. Capital ratios remain strong with a Community Bank Leverage Ratio of 15.69% at June 30, 2025 compared to 13.96% at December 31,2024.

About Broadway Financial Corporation

Broadway Financial Corporation operates through its wholly-owned banking subsidiary, City First Bank, National Association, which is a leading mission-driven bank that serves low-to-moderate income communities within urban areas in Southern California and the Washington, D.C. market.

City First Bank offers a variety of commercial real estate loan products, services, and depository accounts that support investments in affordable housing, small businesses, and nonprofit community facilities located within low-to-moderate income neighborhoods. City First Bank is a Community Development Financial Institution, Minority Depository Institution, Certified B Corp, and a member of the Global Alliance of Banking on Values. The Bank and the City First network of nonprofits, City First Enterprises, Homes By CFE, and City First Foundation, represent the City First branded family of community development financial institutions, which offer a robust lending and deposit platform.

Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0408 ET - London-listed miners fall after the U.S. exempted refined copper from imports tariffs, sending copper prices sliding. The announcement means that refined copper and concentrate won't be subject to a 50% tariff on U.S. imports of semi-finished copper products and copper-intensive derivative products. This sent copper contracts on U.S. commodities exchange Comex tumbling around 22% to $4.368 a pound. In early morning trade, London-listed shares in Chilean copper miner Antofagasta and Rio Tinto fall 4% while Glencore drops 3.5%. Anglo American trades down 2.4%. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

2358 ET - Elite Material's 3Q revenue is likely to grow over 20% from 2Q on stronger artificial-intelligence demand, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. Based on Daiwa's latest supply-chain checks, Amazon Web Services has increased its orders for 'Trainium 2 BBB' chips. Hence, the brokerage now expects Amazon Web Services to account for 25%-30% of the Taiwanese company's 3Q revenue. Together with ongoing volume ramp-up of its '800G' switch, these drivers will probably substantially boost shipments of 'M8' materials by the manufacturer of copper clad laminate and prepreg materials. Daiwa raises the target price to NT$1,225.00 from NT$835.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 10% higher at NT$1,105.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1947 ET - Mineral Resources' share price has more than doubled from a low reached in April. That rally prompts Jefferies to turn bearish on the stock. Analyst Mitch Ryan says pressure remains on Mineral Resources' balance sheet, although the miner has passed a peak of heavy capex and its Onslow project is performing well. "We see continued headwinds in lithium unless further supply comes out, and iron ore with Simandou coming online," says Jefferies. Simandou, owned by Rio Tinto and Chinese companies, is the world's biggest new iron-ore development in Guinea. Jefferies downgrades Mineral Resources to underperform, from hold, but raises its price target by 14% to A$25.00/share. Mineral Resources ended Wednesday at A$30.78. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1944 ET - It's only a matter of time before IGO mothballs both processing units at its Kwinana battery-grade lithium hydroxide refinery, reckons Jefferies. Activity has already stopped on the second unit, or train, at the facility that IGO operates with China's Tianqi under the TLEA joint venture. IGO this week forecast a further impairment of A$70 million-A$90 million on the refinery's assets. "Based on current market conditions and throughput, Train I is not viable as a standalone operation," analyst Mitch Ryan says. "We therefore expect both trains to be placed into care and maintenance within the next 12 months." That would enable investors to have a more direct and transparent exposure to earnings from IGO's Greenbushes lithium operation, Jefferies says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1917 ET [Dow Jones]--Barrenjoey shrugs off Greatland Resources's disappointing guidance. Greatland signaled production in FY 2026 would be some 11% below forecasts in the prospectus for its initial public offering. Analyst Daniel Morgan said the drivers of this downgrade are confined to ore sources at Greatland's Telfer gold mine. "Telfer ore is not the main game for the Greatland investment thesis," Barrenjoey says. Instead, it's ore from the Havieron deposit, which can transform Telfer from a high-cost mine with a short life to an operation with low costs that can sustain mining for much longer. Barrenjoey said the 24% share price fall that followed Greatland's FY 2026 downgrade is more than the 5% reduction in its net present value. It retains an overweight call on Greatland. (david.winning@wsj.com)

1908 ET [Dow Jones]--Lithium miner Liontown Resources is delivering well but Barrenjoey remains bearish on its stock. That's because it has concerns about Liontown's cash flow. Liontown's FY 2026 guidance implies a cash burn of some A$185 million over the next 12 months. That is higher than the company's cash on hand of A$156 million. "Extensions to loan repayments, a further US$100 million of indebtedness and prepayments are levers Liontown is yet to pull, if necessary," analyst Glyn Lawcock says. Barrenjoey has an underweight call on Liontown and A$0.50/share price target. Liontown ended Wednesday at A$0.82. (david.winning@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1300 ET - Research from North Dakota State University, done in partnership with the Department of Agriculture's Agricultural Marketing Service, shows that ports globally have adequate capacity for shipping wheat exports, but the ports of leading wheat exporters Russia and Ukraine have, at times, only limited shipping capacity depending on the time of year. But should Russia invest more into its shipping infrastructure, that could dramatically impact the amount of wheat that ships out of the U.S., says the study. "Expanding Russian port infrastructure in the Baltic and Caspian Sea regions results in a reduction of U.S. exports of between 550,000 to 875,000 metric tons per year, depending on the degree of expansion," the study says. CBOT wheat is down 0.9%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0936 ET - Ferrari's top-line growth has slowed dramatically due to lower sales volumes, says Garrett Nelson, senior equity analyst at CFRA Research. The company now has its work cut out to hit the current EPS consensus of 9.05 euros for 2025, he notes. This is despite Ferrari's history of exceeding expectations, as evidenced by the fact its last quarterly EPS miss was five years ago, he adds. The company's second-quarter adjusted EPS of 2.38 euros was ahead of the 2.31 euros expected by consensus, while net revenue rose 4% to 1.79 billion euros--50 million euros short of consensus. Full-year revenue and adjusted EPS guidance was maintained for at least 7 billion euros and 8.60 euros, respectively. Shares fall 6.2%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0902 ET - Air traffic demand rose in June, but at a slower pace than in previous months, the International Air Transport Association says. Revenue passenger kilometers was up 2.6% last month compared with June 2024. Meanwhile, total capacity, measured in available seat kilometers, was also up 3.4% on year. IATA's Director General Willie Walsh says flight disruptions due to conflicts in the Middle East had an impact on the figures. Load factor stood at 84.5% globally in June, a figure that Walsh describes as still very strong. Load factors are likely to get close to recent historic highs, he adds. European carriers had a 2.8% on-year increase in demand, and a 3.3% capacity boost, according to IATA data. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0819 ET - Ferrari maintained its guidance, but shares could be under pressure as some investors might have been hoping for a rise, RBC Capital Markets analyst Tom Narayan writes. "We don't think this should be read negatively on the remainder of the year, but rather reflects company conservatism," he says. The Italian carmaker has given a floor that it now says it has stronger confidence in reaching, though consensus is already above these levels. RBC views a negative share-price move as a buying opportunity. Ferrari's October investor event will be an important catalyst for shares, as will sales of the F80 and its first electric-vehicle in late 2026. Shares fall 5.5%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0814 ET - BMW delivered a solid second-quarter and confirmed its full-year guidance, as expected, UBS analyst Patrick Hummel writes. The automotive EBIT margin of 5.4% is well within the 5%-7% target range, despite a negative 200 basis-point-tariff impact. Free cash flow at 1.9 billion euros is "very solid," he adds. The investor reaction should be neutral to slightly positive on the strong free-cash-flow generation, Hummel says. However, BMW shares had strongly outperformed over the past few weeks as optimism about the second-quarter numbers and guidance confirmation rose, he says. Shares fall 0.7%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0811 ET - BMW maintained its financial guidance, which at the midpoint would imply consensus numbers might need to move higher, RBC Capital Markets' Tom Narayan writes. On tariffs, BMW is guiding for a 1.25% impact on automotive sales this year, which could be around 1.5 billion euros. "This total is close to Mercedes' guided total." RBC had previously thought BMW had a smaller U.S. tariff exposure versus Mercedes given BMW exports around 180,000 vehicles from Europe to the U.S. compared with around 190,000 for Mercedes. BMW also exports around 110,000 to the EU/U.K from the U.S., versus around 50,000 for Mercedes. BMW's guidance implies a significant stepdown in tariff impact during the second half, likely aided by mitigation and the new U.S.-EU deal, he says. Shares fall 0.7%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0522 ET - Contemporary Amperex Technology will likely post stronger earnings for the next few years, driven by expanding European shipments, a potentially improving domestic pricing outlook and higher operating efficiency, HSBC Global Research analysts write in a note. The European market is likely to make up nearly 20% of CATL's EV battery volumes this year, thanks to a pick up in Europe's EV adoption and the company's scale advantages, they add. The company's 2Q net profit rose 34% on year to CNY16.5 billion, beating HSBC's expectations, boosted by solid shipments and enhanced operating efficiency. HSBC Global Research maintains a buy rating for the stock and raises its target price to CNY363 from CNY322. Shares last closed at CNY264.62.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0445 ET - Aeroports de Paris's first-half earnings came in weaker than expected, but the company remains on track for its full-year guidance, Jefferies says. Recurring Ebitda at the French airport operator missed consensus expectations by 1.3% due to weakness across divisions excluding aviation, Jefferies analysts say in a note. Tax charges and currency headwinds weighed on net profit as well, the analysts say. Nevertheless, the company maintained its guidance for full-year growth of more than 7% in Ebitda. While the weaker-than-expected earnings are likely to put pressure on ADP's shares, the big picture is that the company's guidance is unchanged and it introduced a dividend floor of 3 euros a share for 2025, Jefferies says. Shares fall 4.5%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0443 ET - The dollar is likely to weaken if the Federal Reserve becomes more open to cutting interest rates in the near future, Commerzbank's Volkmar Baur says in a note. Fed Chair Jerome Powell suggested that a rate cut in September was far from certain at Wednesday's meeting where rates were left unchanged. However, Commerzbank continues to expect a cycle of rate cuts to begin in September. The Fed should signal in coming weeks that imminent rate cuts are possible, he says. "This change in communication is likely to weigh on the dollar in the coming weeks, even before the Fed cuts interest rates in September." The DXY dollar index falls 0.1% to 99.718 after hitting a two-month high of 99.983 Wednesday.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0439 ET - European auto-sector stocks provide better value for investors than European auto-sector credit, Deutsche Bank credit strategists say in a note. European auto credit fails to reflect the deterioration in the earnings outlook, leaving bonds vulnerable to declines if the economic backdrop worsens, they say. "Given the backdrop in earnings and equities, auto credit spreads remain at risk of catching-down with fundamentals should the macro backdrop deteriorate and we consider risk-reward rather poor." Investors who are optimistic about the European auto industry are better off investing in the equities rather than credit, the strategists say. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0437 ET - Air France-KLM is making progress on reining in costs although unit costs rose in the second quarter, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. "The second quarter was supposed to be the weakest performance of the year--at the highest part of the low-single-digit range--so a 2.7% increase in unit costs at least looks like starting to get this under control," Alex Irving and Antoine Madre say. Better fuel and cost management helped the airline deliver a better-than-expected EBIT, they say. Latin America has been a strong point for both Lufthansa and Air France-KLM in the quarter, but "Air France-KLM has meaningfully outperformed Lufthansa on the North Atlantic," Bernstein says. Shares trade 3.75% higher at 11.49 euros. (sarah.sloat@wsj.com)

0202 ET - Lufthansa's second-quarter EBIT beat expectations by 4.4%, lifted by associate contributions, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. "The results suggest the beat was due to a surprisingly large profit contribution from [Italian airline] ITA," Alex Irving and Antoine Madre say. The German group's underlying airline profitability isn't obviously improving yet, they say. Second-quarter EBIT was also helped by fuel price tailwinds and the fact that strikes hurt results in the same period a year earlier. Lufthansa Cargo's EBIT surpassed consensus while Lufthansa Technik was below, according to Bernstein. (sarah.sloat@wsj.com)

data.title: Flash flood threat prompts emergency declarations in New York, New Jersey
data.description:
New York Governor Kathy Hochul and her acting counterpart in New Jersey said they were declaring states of emergency for areas facing the threat of extreme flash floods forecast on Thursday for much of the Eastern Seaboard.

The National Weather Service posted flash flood warnings along parts of the Northeast urban corridor stretching from the Washington-Baltimore region north through Philadelphia, Wilmington, New Jersey, and into the New York City metropolitan area.

Severe thunderstorm watches were also in effect across much of the Interstate-95 corridor.

Up to 5 inches (12.7 cm) of rain was forecast in the heaviest bands of showers expected across New York City, Long Island and the Hudson River Valley, with rainfall rates that could exceed 2 inches per hour, according to a statement from Hochul.

"I am urging all New Yorkers to stay vigilant, stay informed, and use caution as we expect excessive rainfall with the potential for flash flooding," Hochul said.

New Jersey was bracing for rainfall totals of 1 to 3 inches generally with localized downpours that could produce 5 to 7 inches, acting New Jersey Governor Tahesha Way said in her declaration.

She warned that the extreme rainfall could trigger landslides, rock slides and flash flooding of roadways across New Jersey, with additional hazards posed by damaging winds from thunderstorms.

"Residents should remain off the roads and indoors unless absolutely necessary," Way, the lieutenant governor, said in a statement. She is temporarily serving as the state's chief executive while Governor Phil Murphy was out of the state on vacation with his family.

The Weather Service attributed the storm threat to a cold front that was bringing a combination of unstable air mass and exceptional amounts of atmospheric moisture to the region.
